summary: Matched several additional Yoshi special-move routines in ftYs_SpecialN.c and adjusted related motion-variable and ft_081B declarations to support better codegen. The main matched bodies were fn_8012D428, ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Anim, ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Phys, and ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Coll. The PR also reshaped Yoshi SpecialN motion vars into a union exposing both a 32-bit word and bitfield bytes, then updated callers from specialn.x0_b0 to specialn.x0.x0_bytes.x0_b0.
what_changed: Implemented fn_8012D428 with ftCommon_8007D5D4 followed by Fighter_ChangeMotionState(gobj, 0x162, 0x0C4C5092U, fp->cur_anim_frame, 0, ftYs_Init_804D9A38, NULL).
Implemented ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Anim: reads GET_FIGHTER(gobj), increments fp->mv.ys.specialn.x4 when held_inputs has bit 0x200 set, and exits to ftCo_800CC730 when ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining returns 0.
Implemented ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Phys as a wrapper around ft_80084EEC with a Fighter_GObj cast.
Implemented ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Coll to branch on fp->mv.ys.specialn.x0.x0: nonzero calls ft_80083A48(gobj, fn_8012E44C), zero calls ft_80082C74(gobj, fn_8012E44C).

Changed Yoshi SpecialN motion vars from a single bitfield member x0_b0 to a union named x0 containing an s32 x0 word and an x0_bytes bitfield struct with x0_b0 through x0_b3, plus added s32 x4 at fp+2344.
Updated existing SpecialN code to access the first bit through fp->mv.ys.specialn.x0.x0_bytes.x0_b0 instead of fp->mv.ys.specialn.x0_b0.
Refined function declarations in ftYs_SpecialN.h: fn_8012D428 now takes Fighter_GObj* and returns void; fn_8012E44C now takes ftYs_GObj* and returns void.
Adjusted ft_081B declarations for matching: ft_80082C74 now takes void* as its first parameter, and ft_80083A48 is declared as void ft_80083A48(void*, HSD_GObjEvent).
Minor comment formatting cleanup in ftYoshiAttributes around added fields and data_filler comments.
smart_moves: Representing SpecialN's fp+2340 storage as a union allowed both whole-word tests via x0.x0 and bit-level stores via x0.x0_bytes.x0_b0, matching different compiler access patterns in nearby functions.
Adding x4 to ftYoshi_SpecialNVars made the held-input counter in ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Anim explicit instead of relying on unnamed or mis-sized storage.
Changing ft_80082C74 and ft_80083A48 first parameters to void* avoided type friction at call sites where ftYs_GObj* or Fighter_GObj* casts affected matching.
Replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with concrete void-returning callback signatures for matched routines improved header accuracy and made the collision callback calls more type-directed.
decomp_lessons: When a motion-var field is accessed sometimes as flags and sometimes as an integer, a union containing a scalar word and a bitfield sub-struct can be a practical matching tool.
Header prototypes are part of matching: changing an argument from Fighter_GObj* to void* or from UNK_PARAMS to a concrete callback signature can affect casts and generated code.
Small wrapper functions for physics/collision often match cleanly once the callee prototype matches the way the original code treated object pointers.
Input flag checks can reveal nearby motion-variable layout; here held_inputs & 0x200 corresponds to incrementing specialn.x4.

Do not assume a PR title is exhaustive: despite being titled SpecialN matches, this slice also matched SpecialAirHi animation, physics, and collision routines within the same Yoshi special source file.
naming_conventions: Yoshi fighter files use the ftYs_ prefix for named routines such as ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Anim, ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Phys, and ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Coll.
Unidentified local routines retained address-derived names such as fn_8012D428 and fn_8012E44C until semantic names are known.
Motion variable field names stayed offset-oriented, using x0, x0_bytes, x0_b0, x0_b1, x0_b2, x0_b3, and x4.
Type aliases distinguish Yoshi-specific object pointers such as ftYs_GObj from generic Fighter_GObj/HSD_GObj use.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted a dummy stack allocation `u8 _[8];` in ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Anim, likely to satisfy stack/register layout for matching; the diff itself provides no further explanation.
Used explicit casts between ftYs_GObj*, Fighter_GObj*, and void* at call sites to align with callee prototypes and expected codegen.
Used numeric constants directly in matched code, including motion state 0x162, flags 0x0C4C5092U, and input mask 0x200.
Branched on the full specialn.x0 word in ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Coll rather than only the x0_b0 bit, enabled by the new union layout.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body states the author also modified ftYoshi SpecialNVars and ft_081B declarations for better matching.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type fn_8012D428 or fn_8012E44C.
Search for other uses of ftYoshi_SpecialNVars.x0.x0 versus x0_bytes to confirm the union layout remained stable.
Check whether ft_80082C74 and ft_80083A48 prototypes were later refined from void* back to specific GObj types.
Look for subsequent ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Coll matching changes around ft_80083A48 and ft_80082C74 callback behavior.
